title: Prior infarct pattern without acute ECG change
patient: A 69-year-old man presents with exertional substernal pressure radiating to the left arm for 90 minutes. He has prior PCI, diabetes, and hypertension. ECG shows old inferior Q waves with no new ST-segment or T-wave changes compared with prior tracing. Troponin I 0.02 ng/mL (reference < 0.04 ng/mL).

must_not_say: old q waves mean active stemi

title: Atypical symptoms with prior Q-wave scar only
patient: A 66-year-old woman reports fatigue and exertional chest heaviness. She has known CAD and hyperlipidemia. ECG demonstrates chronic anterior Q waves from prior infarct with no new ischemic ST/T changes. Troponin I 0.03 ng/mL (reference < 0.04 ng/mL).

title: Low HEART total but troponin greater than three times ULN
patient: A 35-year-old woman has mild epigastric burning and no exertional component, diaphoresis, or radiation. No recognized cardiac risk factors. ECG is normal. Troponin I 0.18 ng/mL (reference < 0.04 ng/mL; about 4.5 times ULN).

title: Atypical chest pain with markedly positive troponin
patient: A 41-year-old man reports intermittent sharp left chest discomfort reproducible with palpation. He has no known atherosclerotic disease and no listed risk factors. ECG has no acute changes. Troponin I 0.13 ng/mL (reference < 0.04 ng/mL; about 3.25 times ULN).

title: Initial workup pending first troponin result
patient: A 53-year-old man presents with pressure-like chest pain with exertion, improved at rest. History of hypertension and smoking. ECG is normal sinus rhythm without acute ischemic changes. Troponin was sent but is still pending.

title: Single negative troponin but serial set incomplete
patient: A 47-year-old woman has intermittent substernal tightness without radiation. She has hyperlipidemia. ECG has nonspecific T-wave flattening. Initial troponin I is 0.02 ng/mL (reference < 0.04 ng/mL), but no repeat value is available yet.

title: Hypotension and altered mentation trigger bypass
patient: A 60-year-old man has crushing chest pain, diaphoresis, confusion, and blood pressure 78/42 mmHg. Prior MI, diabetes, and hypertension. ECG with diffuse ST depression and aVR elevation. Troponin I 0.28 ng/mL (reference < 0.04 ng/mL).

title: Inferior STEMI with reciprocal changes
patient: A 58-year-old woman presents with 30 minutes of severe substernal pressure, diaphoresis, and nausea. ECG shows 2.5 mm ST elevation in II, III, and aVF with reciprocal depression in I and aVL. Troponin I is 0.11 ng/mL (reference < 0.04 ng/mL).

title: Pleuritic pain after long flight with normal ECG
patient: A 29-year-old woman develops sudden pleuritic right-sided chest pain and dyspnea after a 10-hour flight. No recognized HEART risk factors. ECG shows sinus tachycardia without ST/T changes. Troponin I 0.01 ng/mL (reference < 0.04 ng/mL).

title: Pleuritic chest pain with unilateral leg swelling context
patient: A 37-year-old man presents with pleuritic chest pain and mild hemoptysis. He recently had knee surgery and has unilateral calf swelling. ECG is normal sinus rhythm with no ischemic changes. Troponin I 0.02 ng/mL (reference < 0.04 ng/mL).
